content:
# pattern-agent-retro-learning-loop - Overview

Workflow type: retro_learning_loop
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, opencode, Cursor, OpenClaw
Goal: Prevent AI agents from starting from zero every session by establishing a post-project retro loop that captures failures, constraints, and learnings into the database for future agents to retrieve.
Evidence: documented / medium
Source: https://github.com/garrytan/gstack

When to use:
- After every Ship/Release
- After a major failure or unexpected issue
- Weekly team retro for cross-project patterns
- When adding new rules, checklists, or templates

Inputs required:
- Completed project with ship/deployment record
- Access to project logs, error traces, review gate results
- Write access to database rules/wiki/checklists/templates

AI summary: Post-project retrospective learning loop for AI agents. After every Ship, the agent collects successes and failures, distills them into rules/checklists/patterns/templates, writes to the database, rebuilds indexes, and verifies future agents can retrieve the learnings. Prevents agents from starting from zero every session.

# pattern-agent-retro-learning-loop - Execution Steps

Workflow type: retro_learning_loop
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, opencode, Cursor, OpenClaw
Goal: Prevent AI agents from starting from zero every session by establishing a post-project retro loop that captures failures, constraints, and learnings into the database for future agents to retrieve.
Evidence: documented / medium
Source: https://github.com/garrytan/gstack

- Collect: gather what happened during the project (successes, failures, surprises)
- Analyze: identify patterns — what worked, what didn't, what constraints emerged
- Distill: decide what knowledge belongs in rules vs checklists vs patterns vs templates
- Write: update the appropriate database files with new learnings
- Index: rebuild processed chunks so retrieval finds the new knowledge
- Verify: confirm next agent session can retrieve the learnings

AI summary: Post-project retrospective learning loop for AI agents. After every Ship, the agent collects successes and failures, distills them into rules/checklists/patterns/templates, writes to the database, rebuilds indexes, and verifies future agents can retrieve the learnings. Prevents agents from starting from zero every session.

Evidence: documented / medium
Source: https://github.com/garrytan/gstack

- POST /brief or search scripts to verify knowledge retrieval

AI summary: Post-project retrospective learning loop for AI agents. After every Ship, the agent collects successes and failures, distills them into rules/checklists/patterns/templates, writes to the database, rebuilds indexes, and verifies future agents can retrieve the learnings. Prevents agents from starting from zero every session.

# pattern-agent-retro-learning-loop - Prompt Contract

Workflow type: retro_learning_loop
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, opencode, Cursor, OpenClaw
Goal: Prevent AI agents from starting from zero every session by establishing a post-project retro loop that captures failures, constraints, and learnings into the database for future agents to retrieve.
Evidence: documented / medium
Source: https://github.com/garrytan/gstack

- Retro is mandatory after every Ship — not optional
- Learnings must be written to database, not just thought about
- Failure patterns are more valuable than success patterns for future agents
- Cross-project retros identify systemic issues

AI summary: Post-project retrospective learning loop for AI agents. After every Ship, the agent collects successes and failures, distills them into rules/checklists/patterns/templates, writes to the database, rebuilds indexes, and verifies future agents can retrieve the learnings. Prevents agents from starting from zero every session.

Workflow type: retro_learning_loop
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, opencode, Cursor, OpenClaw
Goal: Prevent AI agents from starting from zero every session by establishing a post-project retro loop that captures failures, constraints, and learnings into the database for future agents to retrieve.
Evidence: documented / medium
Source: https://github.com/garrytan/gstack

- Retro entry exists in database
- Search can retrieve recent learnings
- Updated rules/checklists are syntactically valid

AI summary: Post-project retrospective learning loop for AI agents. After every Ship, the agent collects successes and failures, distills them into rules/checklists/patterns/templates, writes to the database, rebuilds indexes, and verifies future agents can retrieve the learnings. Prevents agents from starting from zero every session.

# pattern-agent-retro-learning-loop - Failure Modes

Workflow type: retro_learning_loop
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, opencode, Cursor, OpenClaw
Goal: Prevent AI agents from starting from zero every session by establishing a post-project retro loop that captures failures, constraints, and learnings into the database for future agents to retrieve.

- Skipping retro because project is done
- Writing retro but not rebuilding indexes (knowledge invisible)
- Recording only successes, not failures
- Writing retro in a format agents cannot retrieve

AI summary: Post-project retrospective learning loop for AI agents. After every Ship, the agent collects successes and failures, distills them into rules/checklists/patterns/templates, writes to the database, rebuilds indexes, and verifies future agents can retrieve the learnings. AI summary: Post-project retrospective learning loop for AI agents. # pattern-role-based-agent-review-gates - Overview

Workflow type: review_gate
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, opencode, Cursor, OpenClaw
Goal: Prevent AI agents from skipping design and jumping directly to code by inserting role-based review gates at Plan, Review, Test, and Ship stages with defined checklists, stop conditions, and escalation paths.
Evidence: documented / high
Source: https://github.com/garrytan/gstack

When to use:
- Before any Build phase starts (Plan gates)
- After code is written and before merging (Review gates)
- Before deployment (Test gates)
- Before release (Ship gates)

Inputs required:
- Project type classification (web/backend/automation/desktop)
- Risk level assessment (authentication? payments? user data?)
- Available review resources (single model or cross-model)

AI summary: Role-based review gates for AI agent development: Plan gates (CEO/Eng/Design/DX) prevent skipping design, Review gates (Code/Design/DX/Security) catch bugs, Test gates (QA/Security/Performance) verify quality, Ship gates ensure safe deployment. Failed gates send work back to earlier stages.

# pattern-role-based-agent-review-gates - Execution Steps

Workflow type: review_gate
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, opencode, Cursor, OpenClaw
Goal: Prevent AI agents from skipping design and jumping directly to code by inserting role-based review gates at Plan, Review, Test, and Ship stages with defined checklists, stop conditions, and escalation paths.
Evidence: documented / high
Source: https://github.com/garrytan/gstack

- Plan Gates: CEO (scope) → Eng (architecture) → Design (UI projects) → DX (API projects)
- Review Gates: Code Review → Design Review (UI changes) → DX Review (API changes) → Security Review (high-risk)
- Test Gates: QA (browser for web) → Security Scan (high-risk) → Performance Benchmark (as needed)
- Ship Gates: Ship checklist → Canary Monitor (production) → Document Release
- Failed gates → back to appropriate earlier stage for remediation

AI summary: Role-based review gates for AI agent development: Plan gates (CEO/Eng/Design/DX) prevent skipping design, Review gates (Code/Design/DX/Security) catch bugs, Test gates (QA/Security/Performance) verify quality, Ship gates ensure safe deployment. Failed gates send work back to earlier stages.

# pattern-role-based-agent-review-gates - Prompt Contract

Workflow type: review_gate
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, opencode, Cursor, OpenClaw
Goal: Prevent AI agents from skipping design and jumping directly to code by inserting role-based review gates at Plan, Review, Test, and Ship stages with defined checklists, stop conditions, and escalation paths.
Evidence: documented / high
Source: https://github.com/garrytan/gstack

- Agent must not enter Build without passing required Plan gates
- Cross-model agreement is signal, not proof — user has final say
- Gates are sequential within each stage; all required gates must pass
- Skipping gates requires explicit user override

AI summary: Role-based review gates for AI agent development: Plan gates (CEO/Eng/Design/DX) prevent skipping design, Review gates (Code/Design/DX/Security) catch bugs, Test gates (QA/Security/Performance) verify quality, Ship gates ensure safe deployment. Failed gates send work back to earlier stages.

# pattern-role-based-agent-review-gates - Verification

Workflow type: review_gate
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, opencode, Cursor, OpenClaw
Goal: Prevent AI agents from skipping design and jumping directly to code by inserting role-based review gates at Plan, Review, Test, and Ship stages with defined checklists, stop conditions, and escalation paths.
Evidence: documented / high
Source: https://github.com/garrytan/gstack

- Count of required gates equals count of executed gates
- No P0 issues remain from any gate
- Gate results documented with evidence

AI summary: Role-based review gates for AI agent development: Plan gates (CEO/Eng/Design/DX) prevent skipping design, Review gates (Code/Design/DX/Security) catch bugs, Test gates (QA/Security/Performance) verify quality, Ship gates ensure safe deployment. Failed gates send work back to earlier stages.

# pattern-role-based-agent-review-gates - Failure Modes

Workflow type: review_gate
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, opencode, Cursor, OpenClaw
Goal: Prevent AI agents from skipping design and jumping directly to code by inserting role-based review gates at Plan, Review, Test, and Ship stages with defined checklists, stop conditions, and escalation paths.
Evidence: documented / high
Source: https://github.com/garrytan/gstack

- Agent ignores gate failure and proceeds anyway
- Applying all gates to trivial changes (overhead)
- Skipping Security Gate for projects with auth/payment/user data

AI summary: Role-based review gates for AI agent development: Plan gates (CEO/Eng/Design/DX) prevent skipping design, Review gates (Code/Design/DX/Security) catch bugs, Test gates (QA/Security/Performance) verify quality, Ship gates ensure safe deployment. Failed gates send work back to earlier stages.

- Remaining P0/P1 issues from each gate

AI summary: Role-based review gates for AI agent development: Plan gates (CEO/Eng/Design/DX) prevent skipping design, Review gates (Code/Design/DX/Security) catch bugs, Test gates (QA/Security/Performance) verify quality, Ship gates ensure safe deployment. # pattern-think-plan-build-review-test-ship-reflect - Overview

Workflow type: agentic_software_delivery
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, opencode, Cursor, OpenClaw
Goal: Provide a reusable 7-stage development workflow pattern that any AI agent can follow: Think (redefine problem) → Plan (design + review) → Build (implement) → Review (audit) → Test (QA) → Ship (deploy) → Reflect (learn).
Evidence: documented / high
Source: https://github.com/garrytan/gstack

When to use:
- New feature development
- Major refactoring with architecture changes
- Web projects with UI and browser testing needs
- Any project where code quality matters more than speed

Inputs required:
- Clear problem statement from Think stage
- Architecture design from Plan stage
- Test strategy defined before Build

AI summary: Seven-stage software development pattern for AI agents: Think (redefine problem), Plan (design with review gates), Build (implement with tests), Review (multi-layer audit), Test (browser QA + security), Ship (deploy + canary), Reflect (retro + learn). Each stage has defined inputs, outputs, and stop conditions.

prompt_tags: Think Plan Build Review Test Ship Reflect

content:
# pattern-think-plan-build-review-test-ship-reflect - Execution Steps

Workflow type: agentic_software_delivery
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, opencode, Cursor, OpenClaw
Goal: Provide a reusable 7-stage development workflow pattern that any AI agent can follow: Think (redefine problem) → Plan (design + review) → Build (implement) → Review (audit) → Test (QA) → Ship (deploy) → Reflect (learn).
Evidence: documented / high
Source: https://github.com/garrytan/gstack

- Think: Redefine the real problem; answer 6 mandatory questions; get user confirmation
- Plan: Design architecture with data flow, test matrix, tech choices; pass required review gates (CEO/Eng/Design/DX as applicable)
- Build: Implement per plan with tests; use WIP checkpoints; boil the lake (do the complete thing)
- Review: Multi-layer code review, design audit, DX audit, security audit (as risk requires)
- Test: Browser QA for UI projects, regression tests, security scan for high-risk projects
- Ship: Sync main→test→push→PR→merge→deploy→health check→canary→document
- Reflect: Write retro entry; distill learnings into rules/checklists/templates; update database

AI summary: Seven-stage software development pattern for AI agents: Think (redefine problem), Plan (design with review gates), Build (implement with tests), Review (multi-layer audit), Test (browser QA + security), Ship (deploy + canary), Reflect (retro + learn). Each stage has defined inputs, outputs, and stop conditions.

prompt_tags: Think Plan Build Review Test Ship Reflect

Each stage has defined inputs, outputs, and stop conditions.

content:
# workflow-agent-handoff-report-template - Overview

Workflow type: handoff_report
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, local agents
Goal: Make agents report enough evidence that the user can verify the database was actually used.
Evidence: documented / high
Source: local-generated:2026-04-25/handoff-report

When to use:
- Any task that claims to use the local database
- Frontend generation
- Database-backed research
- Agent handoff between tools

Inputs required:
- Task brief
- Queries run
- Chunks returned
- Files changed
- Verification output

AI summary: When an agent uses the local database, final reports must include API/files read, exact queries, chunk ids, how chunks changed the implementation, files changed, verification, and gaps.

# workflow-agent-handoff-report-template - Execution Steps

Workflow type: handoff_report
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, local agents
Goal: Make agents report enough evidence that the user can verify the database was actually used.
Evidence: documented / high
Source: local-generated:2026-04-25/handoff-report

- Record exactly which local files or API endpoints were read.
- List each query and the key record/chunk ids used.
- Explain how retrieved knowledge affected concrete design or implementation choices.
- List changed files and run commands.
- State verification performed and known gaps.

AI summary: When an agent uses the local database, final reports must include API/files read, exact queries, chunk ids, how chunks changed the implementation, files changed, verification, and gaps.

Evidence: documented / high
Source: local-generated:2026-04-25/handoff-report

- Do not just say 'used the database'; provide query and chunk evidence.
- Keep report concise but auditable.
- Report failures honestly.

AI summary: When an agent uses the local database, final reports must include API/files read, exact queries, chunk ids, how chunks changed the implementation, files changed, verification, and gaps.

# workflow-api-first-ui-search - Overview

Workflow type: api_first_retrieval
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, local agents
Goal: Make agents retrieve relevant UI/design knowledge through the local HTTP API before designing or coding.
Evidence: tested / high
Source: http://127.0.0.1:8765/docs

When to use:
- Any frontend or UI design task
- When the user asks to use the local database
- When the task mentions Liquid Glass, animation, dashboard, forms, tables, or visual style

Inputs required:
- User task brief
- Running API at http://127.0.0.1:8765
- Search keywords extracted from the brief

AI summary: Use API-first retrieval for frontend tasks: call /health, derive search queries, call /ui/search, dedupe relevant chunks, design from those chunks, and report queries/chunks used.

# workflow-api-first-ui-search - Execution Steps

Workflow type: api_first_retrieval
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, local agents
Goal: Make agents retrieve relevant UI/design knowledge through the local HTTP API before designing or coding.
Evidence: tested / high
Source: http://127.0.0.1:8765/docs

- Call /health and confirm references/chunks are available.
- Extract 2-5 search queries from the task, including style, page type, components, and motion terms.
- Call /ui/search for each query with limit 5-8.
- Deduplicate chunks by record_id and section.
- Use only relevant chunks as design constraints; ignore unrelated broad matches.
- Proceed to implementation and cite the chunks used in the final report.

AI summary: Use API-first retrieval for frontend tasks: call /health, derive search queries, call /ui/search, dedupe relevant chunks, design from those chunks, and report queries/chunks used.

# workflow-api-first-ui-search - API Calls

Workflow type: api_first_retrieval
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, local agents
Goal: Make agents retrieve relevant UI/design knowledge through the local HTTP API before designing or coding.
Evidence: tested / high
Source: http://127.0.0.1:8765/docs

- GET http://127.0.0.1:8765/health
- GET http://127.0.0.1:8765/ui/search?q={query}&limit=5
- GET http://127.0.0.1:8765/ui/reference/{record_id}

AI summary: Use API-first retrieval for frontend tasks: call /health, derive search queries, call /ui/search, dedupe relevant chunks, design from those chunks, and report queries/chunks used.

# workflow-api-first-ui-search - Prompt Contract

Workflow type: api_first_retrieval
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, local agents
Goal: Make agents retrieve relevant UI/design knowledge through the local HTTP API before designing or coding.
Evidence: tested / high
Source: http://127.0.0.1:8765/docs

- Do not design purely from memory when the local API is available.
- Do not fetch all references by default; use targeted search.
- Report exact queries and key chunks used.

AI summary: Use API-first retrieval for frontend tasks: call /health, derive search queries, call /ui/search, dedupe relevant chunks, design from those chunks, and report queries/chunks used.

# workflow-gstack-agentic-development-workflow - Overview

Workflow type: agentic_software_delivery
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, opencode, Cursor, OpenClaw
Goal: Establish a Think→Plan→Build→Review→Test→Ship→Reflect 7-stage software delivery pipeline for AI-assisted development, replacing single-prompt code generation with disciplined engineering workflow.
Evidence: documented / high
Source: https://github.com/garrytan/gstack

When to use:
- Starting a new feature or project
- Large refactoring requiring architecture review
- Web projects needing browser QA
- Multi-agent collaboration projects

Inputs required:
- User task description
- Project constraints and platform
- Domain knowledge from /brief or rules

AI summary: Think-Plan-Build-Review-Test-Ship-Reflect 7-stage agentic software delivery pipeline for AI-assisted development. Agent must go through all stages instead of jumping directly to code. Each stage has stop conditions and review gates.

prompt_tags: Think Plan Build Review Test Ship Reflect

# workflow-gstack-agentic-development-workflow - Execution Steps

Workflow type: agentic_software_delivery
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, opencode, Cursor, OpenClaw
Goal: Establish a Think→Plan→Build→Review→Test→Ship→Reflect 7-stage software delivery pipeline for AI-assisted development, replacing single-prompt code generation with disciplined engineering workflow.
Evidence: documented / high
Source: https://github.com/garrytan/gstack

- Think: redefine the problem, answer 6 mandatory questions
- Plan: design architecture, data flow, test matrix; pass required review gates
- Build: implement per plan, write tests, checkpoint commits
- Review: code review, design audit, DX audit, security audit (as applicable)
- Test: browser QA, regression tests, security scan
- Ship: sync main→test→push→PR→merge→deploy→canary
- Reflect: write retro, update rules/checklists/templates in database

AI summary: Think-Plan-Build-Review-Test-Ship-Reflect 7-stage agentic software delivery pipeline for AI-assisted development. Agent must go through all stages instead of jumping directly to code. Each stage has stop conditions and review gates.

content:
# workflow-gstack-agentic-development-workflow - API Calls

Workflow type: agentic_software_delivery
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, opencode, Cursor, OpenClaw
Goal: Establish a Think→Plan→Build→Review→Test→Ship→Reflect 7-stage software delivery pipeline for AI-assisted development, replacing single-prompt code generation with disciplined engineering workflow.
Evidence: documented / high
Source: https://github.com/garrytan/gstack

- POST /brief with workflow_limit > 0
- GET /health to check API availability

content:
# workflow-knowledge-first-frontend-generation - Overview

Workflow type: knowledge_first_frontend
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, frontend agents
Goal: Generate frontend pages using retrieved design knowledge rather than generic template instincts.
Evidence: observed / high
Source: local-generated:2026-04-25/knowledge-first-frontend

When to use:
- Creating a new frontend page
- Redesigning an existing page
- Testing UI database usefulness
- Building style-specific prototypes

Inputs required:
- Product/page brief
- Target tech stack
- Desired visual style if any
- Required components and states

AI summary: For frontend generation, retrieve page/style/component/motion chunks first, synthesize a design brief, implement with existing stack, include states and accessibility, then report how chunks shaped the page.

# workflow-knowledge-first-frontend-generation - Execution Steps

Workflow type: knowledge_first_frontend
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, frontend agents
Goal: Generate frontend pages using retrieved design knowledge rather than generic template instincts.
Evidence: observed / high
Source: local-generated:2026-04-25/knowledge-first-frontend

- Read the task and identify page type, visual style, components, motion needs, and constraints.
- Search UI knowledge for page type, style, component patterns, and motion patterns.
- Create a short design brief from retrieved chunks: layout, components, states, motion, accessibility, avoid rules.
- Implement the page in the existing tech stack without adding dependencies unless explicitly allowed.
- Include meaningful states: loading, empty, error, success, selected, hover/focus where relevant.
- Run available build/static checks and visually inspect if tooling exists.

AI summary: For frontend generation, retrieve page/style/component/motion chunks first, synthesize a design brief, implement with existing stack, include states and accessibility, then report how chunks shaped the page.

# workflow-retrieval-failure-recovery - Overview

Workflow type: failure_recovery
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, local agents
Goal: Recover when local knowledge retrieval fails or returns irrelevant results.
Evidence: observed / high
Source: local-generated:2026-04-25/retrieval-failure

When to use:
- /health fails
- /ui/search returns empty
- Search returns broad unrelated chunks
- Agent cannot access local path
- SQLite index missing

Inputs required:
- Error message
- Query attempted
- API status
- Available scripts

AI summary: If local retrieval fails, check API health, simplify or refine queries, rebuild index if missing, fix Windows/Bash paths, and report fallback evidence instead of silently using memory.

# workflow-retrieval-failure-recovery - Execution Steps

Workflow type: failure_recovery
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, local agents
Goal: Recover when local knowledge retrieval fails or returns irrelevant results.
Evidence: observed / high
Source: local-generated:2026-04-25/retrieval-failure

- If API is down, start backend_api with uvicorn or fall back to search_ui.py.
- If search is empty, simplify query to one core term, then try synonyms.
- If results are broad, add style/page/component constraints or use a more specific query.
- If SQLite DB is missing, run make_chunks.py and build_sqlite_index.py for the relevant domain.
- If direct script path fails on Windows Bash, use forward slashes or call the HTTP API.
- If no knowledge exists, report the gap and optionally create a new reference record after research.

AI summary: If local retrieval fails, check API health, simplify or refine queries, rebuild index if missing, fix Windows/Bash paths, and report fallback evidence instead of silently using memory.

# workflow-virtual-engineering-team-skills - Overview

Workflow type: virtual_engineering_team
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, opencode, Cursor, OpenClaw
Goal: Design multi-role AI agent teams where each role represents a quality checkpoint with defined responsibility boundaries, structured checklists, and deliverable standards — not simulating humans but embedding engineering discipline.
Evidence: documented / high
Source: https://github.com/garrytan/gstack

When to use:
- Complex projects requiring multiple review perspectives
- Projects with UI (design review needed)
- Projects with API (DX review needed)
- Security-sensitive projects (security review needed)
- Team collaboration across multiple agents

Inputs required:
- Project type (web/backend/automation/desktop)
- Risk profile (authentication? payments? user data?)
- Available AI models for cross-model review

AI summary: Multi-role AI agent team design pattern where each role (CEO/Eng/Design/DX/QA/Security/Release) represents a quality checkpoint with defined checklists. Roles are quality gates, not personality simulations. Select roles based on project needs.

# workflow-windows-path-handling-for-agents - Overview

Workflow type: tool_path_windows
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, Bash-based local agents
Goal: Avoid Windows path corruption when agents run commands through Bash or mixed shells.
Evidence: observed / high
Source: local-observed:2026-04-25/bash-windows-path

When to use:
- Agent runs Bash on Windows
- Command includes E:\DataBase paths
- Python cannot open a file because backslashes disappeared

Inputs required:
- Shell type
- Original command
- Error output
- Target file path

AI summary: On Windows, agents using Bash must avoid raw backslash paths. Use E:/DataBase/... or PowerShell. Prefer the local HTTP API to avoid path handling entirely.

# workflow-windows-path-handling-for-agents - Execution Steps

Workflow type: tool_path_windows
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, Bash-based local agents
Goal: Avoid Windows path corruption when agents run commands through Bash or mixed shells.
Evidence: observed / high
Source: local-observed:2026-04-25/bash-windows-path

- Detect whether the tool is Bash, PowerShell, or cmd.
- In Bash/Git Bash, use forward slashes such as E:/DataBase/scripts/ui_design/search_ui.py or /e/DataBase where supported.
- In PowerShell, native Windows paths with backslashes are safe.
- Quote paths with spaces and avoid unescaped backslashes in Bash.
- If path corruption occurs, rerun the command with forward slashes.

AI summary: On Windows, agents using Bash must avoid raw backslash paths. Use E:/DataBase/... or PowerShell. Prefer the local HTTP API to avoid path handling entirely.

# workflow-windows-path-handling-for-agents - Prompt Contract

Workflow type: tool_path_windows
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, Bash-based local agents
Goal: Avoid Windows path corruption when agents run commands through Bash or mixed shells.
Evidence: observed / high
Source: local-observed:2026-04-25/bash-windows-path

- Do not use raw Windows backslash paths in Bash commands.
- Prefer local API calls over direct script paths when the API is running.

AI summary: On Windows, agents using Bash must avoid raw backslash paths. Use E:/DataBase/... or PowerShell. Prefer the local HTTP API to avoid path handling entirely.
